title: Photosynthesis Basics
content: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight, carbon dioxide, and water to produce glucose and oxygen. Chlorophyll absorbs light energy, mainly from red and blue wavelengths.

title: Cell Structure
content: The nucleus controls cell activities and stores genetic material. Mitochondria release energy through cellular respiration. Ribosomes help synthesize proteins.

title: Acids and Bases
content: Acids release hydrogen ions in solution, while bases release hydroxide ions or accept hydrogen ions. The pH scale measures how acidic or basic a substance is.

title: Chemical Bonding
content: Ionic bonds form through transfer of electrons, usually between metals and nonmetals. Covalent bonds form when atoms share electron pairs.

title: Newton's Laws
content: Newton's first law states that an object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force. The second law relates force, mass, and acceleration through F = ma.

title: Work and Energy
content: Work is done when a force causes displacement. Kinetic energy is the energy of motion, and potential energy is stored energy due to position or configuration.

title: Quadratic Equations
content: A quadratic equation has the standard form ax^2 + bx + c = 0. Solutions can be found using factoring, completing the square, or the quadratic formula.

title: Probability Fundamentals
content: Probability measures the likelihood of an event and ranges from 0 to 1. For equally likely outcomes, probability equals favorable outcomes divided by total outcomes.

title: Supervised Learning
content: Supervised learning uses labeled data to train models for prediction tasks such as classification and regression. The model learns a mapping from inputs to outputs.

title: Vector Databases
content: Vector databases store embedding vectors and support similarity search. In retrieval-augmented generation systems, they help find the most relevant context for a user query.
